name: San Onofre / Old Man’s / Dogpatch
direction: rights and lefts; soft, slow, slopey
bestSeason: summer–fall for longboard-friendly S/SW swells; winter can be cleaner/consistent
skillNotes: Classic mellow longboard zone; Dogpatch is especially soft/beginner-friendly; crowded but generally forgiving.
whyItBelongs: One of California’s defining longboard waves: trim, glide, noseride-friendly shoulders.

name: Malibu Surfrider
skillLevel: intermediate to advanced because of crowd/positioning
skillNotes: Technically perfect but extremely crowded and competitive; etiquette is critical.
whyItBelongs: Iconic long right walls, trim sections, and noseride potential; a global longboard benchmark.

name: Rincon
skillNotes: World-class and sectiony; positioning, current, rocks, crowd pressure, and speed matter.
whyItBelongs: Long point-wave flow with high-line trimming and classic California longboard history.

name: Cardiff Reef
skillNotes: Reef and crowd awareness needed; softer on smaller days, more serious with size.
whyItBelongs: Long, open reef shoulders with trim-friendly walls and strong longboard culture.

name: Pleasure Point / Cowells
breakType: reef/point setup; Cowells is a sheltered soft point
direction: mostly rights at Pleasure Point; Cowells has soft rights/inside rollers
bestSeason: fall–winter; Cowells best when enough west swell wraps in
skillLevel: beginner-friendly at Cowells; intermediate at Pleasure Point
skillNotes: Cowells is beginner/longboard friendly but crowded; Pleasure Point has more reef, current, and local crowd pressure.
whyItBelongs: Santa Cruz longboard staple with long rights, playful sections, and historic surf culture.

name: Church
skillNotes: Softer than Lowers, but still has rocks and crowd pressure; good for midlengths and longboards.
whyItBelongs: More forgiving Trestles-area wave with long, lined-up shoulders on south swell.

name: C Street
skillNotes: Long and rippable but crowded; current, rocks, and stronger winter surf require experience.
whyItBelongs: Long right walls and multiple takeoff zones make it a strong longboard/midlength point-wave entry.

name: Blackies
direction: mostly lefts with point influence; mixed peaks depending sand
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Friendly longboard scene but can be crowded; rips and winter punch need respect.
whyItBelongs: Newport’s classic small-wave longboard hangout with soft, easy rollers when modest.

name: Swami’s
skillNotes: Quality wave with rocks and heavy crowd/local presence; not ideal for true beginners.
whyItBelongs: Long, clean reef rights with excellent trim and turn sections for skilled longboarders.

name: Doheny
skillNotes: Soft, slow, very approachable; crowded with learners and longboards; watch rocks and water quality after rain.
whyItBelongs: Gentle peelers and forgiving takeoffs make it one of SoCal’s best beginner longboard waves.

name: Tourmaline
bestSeason: summer–fall S/SW swells; winter can be consistent
skillNotes: Classic mellow longboard and fish wave; crowded but generally less high-performance than nearby reefs.
whyItBelongs: San Diego’s archetypal longboard playground: soft peaks, easy takeoffs, trim sections.

name: Mondos
skillNotes: Very mellow learning/longboard wave; can be crowded when small and clean.
whyItBelongs: Soft, slow rollers near Ventura that suit gliding, learners, and relaxed longboard sessions.

name: Kitty Hawk Pier
bestSeason: summer for consistency; fall for cleaner tropical/groundswell windows
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Public OBX pier zone; can get crowded, with rips and pier hazards.
whyItBelongs: Reliable, accessible Outer Banks beachbreak with rideable left/right sandbar peaks.

name: Jennette’s Pier
skillNotes: Very public, easy-access Nags Head spot; watch pier/current zones.
whyItBelongs: Recognizable public OBX setup; sandbars can produce forgiving, makeable shoulders.

name: Kill Devil Hills / Avalon Pier zone
bestSeason: fall–winter; small summer windswell can be longboardable
skillNotes: Classic public OBX town beach feel; crowds possible near better sandbars and piers.
whyItBelongs: Realistic longboard seed candidate when sandbars line up.

name: S-Turns / Rodanthe
skillNotes: More exposed OBX energy; currents, shifting bars, and crowd pressure when good.
whyItBelongs: Publicly known Hatteras Island sandbar zone with peeling potential when small-to-medium and organized.

name: Avon Pier
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on smaller days
skillNotes: Public pier zone; more manageable than heavier Hatteras spots when small, but still exposed to OBX currents.
whyItBelongs: Known Hatteras Island pier setup that can offer long, softer sandbar shoulders.

name: Old Lighthouse / Buxton
skillNotes: Not a mellow beginner spot with size; small, clean, lined-up days can peel.
whyItBelongs: One of NC’s iconic public surf zones with strong sandbar potential.

name: Frisco Pier / Frisco
breakType: beach / pier break, somewhat more sheltered depending wind/swell
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Public Hatteras south-facing zone; watch shifting sand and structure/current context.
whyItBelongs: Often a mellower Hatteras option for clean, smaller longboard runners.

name: Wrightsville Beach / Oceanic–Crystal–Mercer pier zones
direction: mixed peaks; often lefts/rights depending bar
skillNotes: Highly accessible surf-town vibe, popular/crowded, with lots of learners and longboarders.
whyItBelongs: One of the obvious southern NC longboard seeds: public, consistent enough, established surf community.

name: Masonboro Island / Masonboro Inlet
skillNotes: Access is less casual than town beaches; boat/kayak/paddle logistics and changing inlet currents matter.
whyItBelongs: Publicly listed NC beachbreak with right/left waves and less dense crowd than central Wrightsville.

name: Surf City Pier / Topsail Island
bestSeason: fall–winter; small summer windswells for logging
skillNotes: Public pier-town setup; family beach vibe, can get busy around the pier.
whyItBelongs: Realistic southern/central NC longboard card: softer beachbreak peaks, public access, small-wave days.

name: The Washout, Folly Beach
bestSeason: fall–winter; tropical season can produce quality windows
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days; intermediate when bigger/crowded
skillNotes: Public Charleston-area surf zone; busy and variable.
whyItBelongs: Strong nearby East Coast addition with friendly sandbar shoulders in clean smaller surf.

name: Cocoa Beach Pier / Cocoa Beach
bestSeason: winter and hurricane season; summer often small but longboardable
skillNotes: Very public, established surf/longboard/learner scene; crowds common but approachable.
whyItBelongs: Archetypal East Coast longboard-friendly town wave: soft, accessible, public, small-wave famous.
